Defendant Albay Construction Company's motion for summary judgment.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Nov-04-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Defendant Moore Dry Dock Company's motion for summary judgment.

APPEARANCES: Oren P. Noah, Esq. of Brayton Purcell LLP 415/898-1555 for pltfs & opposing parties; Elizabeth N. Branham, Esq. of Lewis Brisbois Bisgaard & Smith LLP 415/438-6682 for deft & moving party Moore Dry Dock Company.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - Defendant Moore Dry Dock Company's motion for summary judgment is denied. As to the exposure issue, defendant sustained its initial burden. Mr. Tiner's deposition testimony and declaration and Charles Ay's declaration create a triable issue whether plaintiff Glen Tiner was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (pipe insulation) attributable to defendant. This secondary exposure occurred through plaintiff's father. Plaintiffs do not dispute that defendant is not liable for any alleged exposure to asbestos incurred as a result of Glen Tiner's direct exposure to asbestos while an employee of Moore Dry Dock. As to the duty issue, the declarations of Glen Tiner and Dr. Richard Cohen and the U.S. Maritime Commission document create a triable issue whether defendant owed a duty to Glen Tiner. Specifically, they create a triable issue whether defendant knew or reasonably should have known that the installation, removal, use, disturbance and/or cleaning up of asbestos-containing products or materials could create a safety hazard of secondary exposure for a member of an employee's household. Pursuant to Rowland v. Christian (1968) 69 Cal.2d 108 and Campbell v. Ford Motor Company (2012) 206 Cal.App.4th15, public policy dictates in favor of a creation of a duty in the present matter given that Francis Tiner was employed by defendant. Defendant's objections to Mr. Tiner's declaration, Mr. Ay's declaration and Dr. Cohen's declaration are overruled. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Tara L. Williams of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #13428 (510/451-1580) (503/TLJ)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Oct-30-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Defendant Flowserve Corp.'s motion for summary judgment.

APPEARANCES: Oren P. Noah, Esq. of Brayton Purcell LLP 415/898-1555 for pltfs & opposing parties; no appearance by moving party defendant Flowserve Corp.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling - Defendant Flowserve Corp.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Tiner was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Specifically, defendant's separate statement fails to lay out undisputed facts demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that the Durametallic packing to which Mr. Tiner was exposed contained asbestos. Plaintiffs' objections to Robert Smith's declaration are overruled but his declaration still fails to negate an essential element of plaintiffs' claim, i.e., the asbestos content of the Durametallic packing. Defendant's objections to Mr. Tiner's deposition testimony are overruled. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Denise M. Lombardo of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #5419 (510/451-1580) (503/TLJ)

APPEARANCES: Oren P. Noah, Esq. of Brayton Purcell LLP 415/898-1555 for pltfs & opposing parties; Tomas S. Glaspy, Esq. of Law Offices of Glaspy & Glaspy, Inc. 925/947-1300 for deft & moving party LG&E and KU Capital LLC.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - Defendant LG&E and KU Capital LLC's motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Tiner was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant is precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar because it did not demonstrate that plaintiffs failed to provide meaningful responses to comprehensive interrogatories designed to elicit all the evidence plaintiffs have to support their contention of liability against defendant. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Denise M. Lombardo of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #5419 (510/451-1580) (503/TLJ)
